title: mildella fallax
side_effects: Mildella fallax can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues due to its volatile oils. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to skin burns, blisters, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. Ingesting the plant can result in stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting, as well as interact with medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions. In severe cases, improper use of Mildella fallax can cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
